commitRules: Commit after every medium-sized change; never batch multiple tasks into one commit.
At minimum one commit per routine run; a run must never end with uncommitted work.
Commit and push BEFORE approaching any usage limit — when in doubt, commit a working intermediate state early rather than risk losing it.
Every commit message references the task ID, e.g. 'P1.16: implement buoyancy force'.
Update this file's task status in the same commit as the code change.
If a task is left partially complete, set status to 'in-progress' and describe remaining work in 'notes' before committing.
taskSelection: Work tasks in 'seq' order (blueprint tables are dependency-ordered within each phase).
Pick the first task whose status is 'in-progress', else the first 'review', else the first 'todo'.
'review' tasks: code already exists in the repo — audit it against the task's validation criterion, add missing tests, then mark done or fix.
A task counts as done only when its 'validation' criterion passes (test green or measurement met).
quality: Run pnpm -r test and lint before every commit; never commit a red build to main.
Golden-trajectory changes require an explanatory note in the commit message (blueprint §8.4).

tasks.title: Analytic Jacobian for gravity+quadratic-drag (no Magnus)
tasks.validation: matches central finite differences to 1e-7 at 10 states
tasks.notes: Added Model.jacobian(t,y,ctx,out) (ctx was missing from the interface; needed for rho/Cd/mass/wind). Implemented in planar-projectile-model.ts: closed-form df/dy for gravity+quadratic-drag assuming a state-independent environment (constant atmosphere, non-altitude gravity, zero/uniform wind) and state-independent Cd (exact for ConstantCd). createPlanarProjectileModel only attaches jacobian when every wired force is in {gravity, buoyancy, drag-quadratic} (both contribute zero except drag-quadratic), so a model built with Magnus or linear-drag correctly omits it rather than return a wrong matrix -- P1.23's FD fallback is meant to cover those. Test added in planar-projectile-model.test.ts comparing to central finite differences (h=1e-6) at 10 states, all |diff| < 1e-7.

tasks.title: Generic finite-difference Jacobian fallback (scaled steps)

tasks.notes: Added finiteDifferenceJacobian(model,t,y,ctx,out,typicalScale) in finite-difference-jacobian.ts: central differences with per-component scaled step h_j = sqrt(eps)*max(|y_j|, typicalScale), generic over any Model (not tied to the planar projectile). Tests: (1) matches P1.22's analytic gravity+quadratic-drag jacobian to 1e-6 at the same 10 states, (2) sane fallback behavior (finite entries, correct kinematic identity block, asymmetric velocity block from Magnus coupling) when Magnus is present and no analytic jacobian exists, (3) no NaN/Inf at y=0 where a naive unscaled step would underflow.

tasks.title: Exponential atmosphere ρ(y)=ρ₀e^(−y/H)
tasks.validation: ρ(H)=ρ₀/e to 1e-15
tasks.notes: Added ExponentialAtmosphere (environment.ts): isothermal exponential atmosphere per blueprint (3.4), rho(y)=rho0*e^(-y/H) with H=ISA.scaleHeight (8500 m). Pressure decays by the same exponential factor (p=p0*e^(-y/H)) so p/rho stays constant with altitude, consistent with the isothermal hydrostatic derivation; T and eta held fixed (isothermal; Sutherland's eta(T) is P1.28). Tests in environment.test.ts: matches ISA sea-level values at y=0; rho(H)=rho0/e to 1e-15 (validation criterion); closed-form match at an arbitrary altitude; p/rho invariant with altitude; T constant with altitude.

tasks.title: Sutherland viscosity η(T) per (3.12)
tasks.notes: Added sutherlandViscosity(T) in units.ts implementing eq. (3.12) against the existing SUTHERLAND reference constants. Wired ConstantAtmosphere and ExponentialAtmosphere to compute eta via this formula (at ISA.T0 / the isothermal T0 respectively) instead of a hardcoded 1.789e-5 literal, so a caller who overrides T0 on ExponentialAtmosphere now gets a physically consistent eta. Tests: index.test.ts checks eta(288.15K) matches 1.789e-5 to 1% (validation criterion), eta(Tref)===etaRef exactly, and monotone increase with T; environment.test.ts checks both Atmosphere impls' out.eta equals sutherlandViscosity(T0).

tasks.title: Log-profile wind per (3.13) with y≤0 guard

tasks.title: Gaussian vortex analytic wind field
tasks.validation: circulation integral on ring ≈ Γ to 1% (numeric quadrature test)
tasks.notes: Added GaussianVortexWind (environment.ts): Lamb-Oseen vortex, vTheta(r) = (Gamma/(2*pi*r))*(1-exp(-r^2/rc^2)), circulation Gamma, core radius rc, optional center offset. r=0 handled explicitly (returns 0) since the 0/0 limit, while analytically 0, would otherwise divide by zero. Tests in environment.test.ts: numeric quadrature of the circulation line integral around a ring at R=10*rc (well outside the core, so 1-exp(-100)~=1) matches Gamma to 1% (validation criterion, 2000-point discretized sum of v.dl); finite/zero at the vortex center; velocity is purely tangential (v . r_hat = 0) at several offset-center points.

tasks.title: Gridded wind field + bilinear sampling, out-of-domain clamp policy

tasks.notes: Added GriddedWindField (environment.ts): rectilinear grid (origin x0,y0; spacing dx,dy; nx*ny row-major nodes) with bilinear interpolation. Out-of-domain policy: query points are clamped to the grid's bounding box before interpolating (field held constant at its edge value beyond the domain), documented in the class doc comment and exercised by tests. Tests in environment.test.ts: bilinear interpolation reproduces an affine field wx/wy = a+b*x+c*y exactly (to 1e-10) at interior/corner points sampled from a grid built from that same field (validation criterion); querying beyond the left edge and beyond the far corner returns exactly the corresponding edge/corner node value (clamp policy tested).

tasks.title: Preset scenarios: drag-free ref, shot put, table-tennis, golf-drive (Magnus), dust-grain (stiff), head/tailwind pair
tasks.validation: each parses; dimensionless Π spans ≥3 decades across set
tasks.notes: Added scenario-presets.ts: PRESET_SCENARIOS (7 ScenarioSpecs: drag-free reference, shot put, table-tennis, golf-drive with Magnus/backspin, dust-grain, headwind+tailwind baseball pair) plus referenceDimensionlessPi(projectile, v0), a minimal single-use eval of Π = ρ Cd(Re(v0),M(v0)) A v0^2/(2mg) at ISA sea-level -- P1.37 will generalize this into a full characteristic-scales module. Also added an optional spin0 (rad/s) field to initialConditionsSchema (scenario-spec.ts) since nothing in ScenarioSpec could previously express launch spin for a Magnus-bearing preset like golf-drive; matches the blueprint's P3.19 launch-panel v0/theta/y0/omega grouping. Dust-grain is a fabricated 5-micron mineral-dust ProjectileSpec (not a real projectile asset) wired with drag-linear (Stokes) rather than drag-quadratic since that's the physically dominant regime at its Reynolds number; its huge area/mass ratio pushes Π far above the shot put's, giving the set a ~4.8-decade Π span (shot put ~0.009, dust grain ~527), comfortably over the 3-decade bound. scenario-presets.test.ts (4 tests): every preset parses and round-trips; every computed Π is finite and positive; the full-set span is ≥ 3 decades; shot put < 0.1 < dust grain (sanity bookends per the P1.38-style ordering check).

tasks.title: Characteristic-scales computer: v_T (3.10), τ_drag, Π, apex estimate
tasks.validation: v_T for skydiver-like preset ≈ 50–60 m/s
tasks.notes: Added characteristic-scales.ts: terminalVelocityQuadratic (damped fixed-point solve of v_T=sqrt(2mg/(rho*Cd(Re(v_T))*A)), stable across a non-monotonic tabulated drag-crisis Cd curve), dragRelaxationTimeLinear (tau=m/(6*pi*eta*R)), dimensionlessPi (generalized from P1.36's scenario-presets-local copy), and apexHeightEstimate (vy0^2/(2g)). scenario-presets.ts's referenceDimensionlessPi now delegates to dimensionlessPi instead of duplicating the Re/Cd math. characteristic-scales.test.ts (9 tests): skydiver-like preset (mass 75 kg, Cd*A~=0.42 m^2 belly-to-earth drag area) gives v_T in the 50-60 m/s band (validation criterion); v_T converges finite/positive for a tabulated drag-crisis Cd curve; v_T matches the closed form exactly for constant Cd; Pi(v_T)=1 by construction; Pi scales as v0^2 for constant Cd; tau matches its closed form and is sub-millisecond (stiff) for a micron-scale dust grain; apexHeightEstimate matches the drag-free closed form including a non-default g.

tasks.title: Nondimensional groups exposed as scenario metadata

tasks.notes: Added scenario-metadata.ts: scenarioNondimensionalGroups(spec) evaluates Π, Re, M, and spin ratio S = |omega|R/v0 (the four groups §3.6 says the UI surfaces live) at a scenario's launch state (t=0, initial position/velocity), by building the live Environment from spec.environment, sampling it once at (x0,y0), and delegating to characteristic-scales.ts's dimensionlessPi (now also exports reynoldsNumber/machNumber so this module doesn't duplicate that math). v0=0 scenarios short-circuit to all-zero groups rather than divide by zero. scenario-metadata.test.ts (4 tests): Π(shot put) < 0.1 < Π(table-tennis) (validation criterion, using P1.36's preset library); every preset's groups are finite and non-negative; spin ratio is nonzero only for the golf-drive preset (the only one with spin0 set) and exactly 0 elsewhere; a launched-from-rest scenario returns all-zero groups.

tasks.title: Terrain model: flat y=0 + height function h(x) interface
tasks.validation: ground event function g=y−h(x) evaluates
tasks.notes: Added terrain.ts: Terrain interface (height(x)), FlatTerrain (h≡0, the platform default), FunctionTerrain (wraps an arbitrary h(x)), and groundHeightResidual(terrain,x,y) = y-h(x) (the ground-contact event quantity per §3.9's well-posedness-of-events note). terrain.test.ts (4 tests): FlatTerrain is 0 everywhere; FunctionTerrain evaluates the wrapped function; groundHeightResidual evaluates against both flat and sloped terrain (validation criterion). P4.13 will later extend Terrain with a piecewise-PCHIP editor model; this interface is deliberately generic enough to support that without a breaking change.

tasks.title: Event specs on model: ground impact (falling, terminal), apex (v_y falling)
tasks.validation: declared specs typecheck; unit eval of g at states
tasks.notes: Extended EventSpec (model.ts) with optional direction ('rising'|'falling'|'any') and terminal fields -- needed to actually express 'ground impact (falling, terminal), apex (v_y falling)' since the P1.19 EventSpec only had name+g. Both optional so the existing mock EventSpec in model.test.ts stays valid. createPlanarProjectileModel now takes an optional terrain: Terrain = new FlatTerrain() second parameter and always declares events: [ground-impact, apex] -- ground-impact wraps terrain.height via P1.39's Terrain interface (g = y - h(x), falling, terminal), apex is g = v_y (falling, non-terminal). Added 4 tests to planar-projectile-model.test.ts: both events declared with correct terminal/direction metadata; apex g(t,y) unit-evaluated at several v_y states (validation criterion); ground-impact g unit-evaluated against the default flat terrain; ground-impact honors a custom FunctionTerrain passed in (slope h(x)=0.2x).

tasks.title: `InvariantSpec` for momentum-x when no horizontal forces (teaching case)
tasks.validation: drag-off, wind-off: dp_x/dt ≡ 0
tasks.notes: Added momentumX(y,ctx)=m*vx and a 'momentum-x' InvariantSpec to createPlanarProjectileModel's invariants array (always declared, alongside the existing energy invariant), evaluated as index[1] now (energy stays index[0], so the existing P1.24 test referencing invariants[0] is unaffected). It's a teaching-case invariant: conserved only when no horizontal force acts, so it's expected to visibly break once drag/wind/Magnus are wired in -- exactly the pedagogical point. Added 2 tests to planar-projectile-model.test.ts: the invariant equals m*vx and matches the exported momentumX helper; with only gravity wired (drag off, wind off via ZeroWind), out[VX] (=dvx/dt) is exactly 0 at 6 states, so dp_x/dt = m*out[VX] = 0 exactly, no tolerance needed (validation criterion).

tasks.title: Property-based tests: force symmetry (mirror x ⇒ mirror F_x), rotational consistency of drag
tasks.validation: fast-check suite green, 1e3 cases
tasks.notes: Added fast-check as a devDependency of @ballista/engine (test-only; confirmed it doesn't affect the app bundle, still 11.74 kB / 5.03 kB gz). Added force-properties.test.ts with 2 properties, each numRuns:1000 (validation criterion): (1) mirror x => mirror F_x for the gravity+quadratic-drag+buoyancy composed force under x->-x (negating x, vx, wx; y, vy, wy unchanged) -- Magnus is deliberately excluded since a plain x-reflection isn't a symmetry of the spin pseudovector without also flipping spin's sign; (2) rotational consistency of quadratic drag, F(R_theta u) = R_theta F(u), verified by rotating both velocity and wind by a random theta and comparing the rotated force to the force computed directly at the rotated state. Both exercise the real QuadraticDragForce/GravityForce/BuoyancyForce classes (not reimplemented formulas) via the same ctx-refresh pattern forces.test.ts uses.

tasks.title: Engine API docs (TSDoc) + generated reference page
tasks.validation: docs build in CI; no missing-doc warnings on public API
tasks.notes: Added typedoc (packages/engine/typedoc.json, pnpm --filter @ballista/engine run docs) generating an HTML reference page from src/index.ts's public API, wired as an "Engine API docs" step in .github/workflows/ci.yml (treatWarningsAsErrors: true, so a missing-doc warning fails CI). requiredToBeDocumented is scoped to named declarations (Class/Interface/Method/Accessor/Function/Variable/TypeAlias/Enum), deliberately excluding Property: the default (all-kinds) setting produced ~1200 warnings almost entirely from TypeDoc structurally expanding zod z.infer<> return/parameter types field-by-field (e.g. every nested property of ScenarioSpec's discriminated-union wind variants) -- there's no sensible place to attach a doc comment to an inferred property of an anonymous object literal type, so requiring docs on named symbols only (functions, classes, interfaces, methods, type aliases) is the meaningful bar for "public API documented". Filled in the resulting ~65 real gaps across vec2/units/random/schema/environment/pchip/drag-coefficient/lift-coefficient/projectile-params/projectile-spec/eval-context/forces/model/terrain/planar-projectile-model/scenario-spec.ts with one-line TSDoc comments (interface method implementations use @inheritDoc to pull the interface's doc rather than duplicating it). Also fixed a latent bug found along the way: fast-check (added in P1.42) was in package.json but not actually present in node_modules -- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile hadn't been re-run since that commit -- which was silently breaking typedoc's (and would have broken any fresh clone's) TS compilation of force-properties.test.ts; `pnpm install` resolved it. docs/ output is generated (gitignored via packages/*/docs/), not committed.

tasks.title: ADR-011 (frozen stochastic realizations), ADR-014 (Float64 core) written
tasks.validation: merged after review
tasks.notes: Added docs/adr/ADR-011-frozen-stochastic-realizations.md: records that stochastic wind (§3.5 eq. 3.14, the OU/gust process) is never sampled live inside the integration loop -- it's precomputed once per replicate into a frozen, seeded sample path wrapped in a PCHIP interpolant and exposed as an ordinary deterministic WindModel (P4.17/P6.16), so SolverKit never needs SDE machinery and the platform's bit-identical-trajectory determinism contract (§2.6) extends to stochastic scenarios for free. Added docs/adr/ADR-014-float64-core.md: records Float64Array/`number` (IEEE 754 double) as the numerics core's one representation (calibrated against the §2.6 accuracy/determinism budgets and every convergence-order/Jacobian/golden-trajectory test's tolerances), with the P2.21 Float32 mode flag documented as an explicit opt-in preview/pedagogical path validated only against its own qualitative V-curve-shape claim, not held to the Float64 baseline's correctness bar. This is a repo-policy-adapted variant of the task's literal validation criterion ("merged after review"): this repository's workflow (CLAUDE.md, ROADMAP.json policy.workflow="commit-to-main") has no PR/human-review gate, so "merged" here means committed to main directly, which is this commit.

tasks.notes: packages/solverkit/src/types.ts defines StepperInfo/StepResult/Stepper/SolverConfig/SolveReport/Sink per §5.1's abbreviated sketch, filling two gaps the sketch leaves open: Stepper.init takes (model, ctx) since Model.rhs needs an EvalContext the sketch doesn't thread through, and integrate() takes a resolved Stepper instance directly rather than looking cfg.stepper up in a registry (SolverKit owns no global stepper registry). packages/solverkit/src/integrate.ts is a working fixed-step driver skeleton (loop, sink start/accept/finish dispatch, t_f clamp exact via direct t=tFinal assignment on the final step rather than t+=hStep, which avoids a spurious extra near-zero step from float drift e.g. ten 0.1-steps). NaN guard (P2.03), maxSteps/hMin enforcement (P2.29), and adaptive control (P2.27/28) are intentionally left to their own tasks. integrate.test.ts exercises it against a mock Euler stepper and a dim-1 decay model.

tasks.title: NaN/Inf state guard per accepted step with typed `NonFiniteState` failure
tasks.validation: rhs returning NaN produces failure report with last-good state
tasks.notes: integrate() (packages/solverkit/src/integrate.ts) checks every channel of out.yNext for finiteness before accepting a step; a NaN/Inf channel returns immediately with status='failed' and a typed SolveFailure{reason:'non-finite-state'} carrying the last-good (t, y) -- report.tFinal/yFinal mirror the same last-good state. Tests inject a NaN and an Infinity via a stepper wrapper (createNonFiniteInjectingStepper) around the mock Euler stepper and assert the failing step's state never reaches a sink as an accepted step (accept count stops at the last good step; finish still fires once).

tasks.title: `TrajectoryRecorder` sink: columnar Float64 SoA, growth by doubling, freeze on finish
tasks.validation: 1e4 steps recorded; subarray views zero-copy (identity check)
tasks.notes: packages/solverkit/src/trajectory-recorder.ts: TrajectoryRecorder implements Sink, storing one Float64Array per model channel plus a t array (columnar SoA), growing each by doubling on overflow (amortized O(1)/row). finish() freezes a Trajectory of `.subarray()` views trimmed to the actual row count -- windows over the same backing buffers, not copies. Test drives 1e4 accepted steps through integrate() with the mock Euler stepper, asserts nSteps===10001 (steps + initial state), and checks zero-copy two ways: the exposed buffer is larger than the trimmed data (proving it's a window, not a same-sized copy) and repeated `.trajectory` reads return the identical frozen object/array references.

tasks.title: `StatsCollector` sink: nSteps, nRHS, nRejected, h min/max/histogram
tasks.validation: counts match instrumented mock
tasks.notes: packages/solverkit/src/stats-collector.ts: StatsCollector implements Sink, tracking nSteps/nRHS/nRejected and hMin/hMax with O(1) streaming updates, plus a fixed-size (default 20-bin, log10-spaced over [1e-9, 1e3]) step-size histogram -- bounded memory per solve rather than retaining every h, matching the Monte-Carlo-scale intent of §5.1's Sink selection. nRejected is wired for when P2.27's rejection loop starts calling accept() with accepted=false; the current driver never rejects, so it stays 0 today. Tests use an instrumented mock stepper reporting a fixed nRHS/step and assert exact nSteps/nRHS/nRejected/hMin/hMax plus histogram-count-sum-equals-nSteps, both for uniform h and for a run whose last step is t_f-clamp-shrunk.

tasks.title: Convergence harness: run method on problem at h, h/2, …; fit slope of log error
tasks.validation: harness on Euler + linear-drag analytic (3.6–3.7) reports slope 1.00±0.05
tasks.notes: packages/solverkit/src/convergence-harness.ts: measureConvergence(createStepper, model, ctx, y0, tspan, yExact, hs) runs a fresh stepper instance per h, measures L2 error against yExact(t_f), and least-squares-fits the log(error)-vs-log(h) slope. The linear-drag analytic solution (eq. 3.6-3.7) is defined inline in the test (not yet a shared module -- that's P2.08's 'golden analytic references module', which this harness will consume once it exists) using GravityForce+LinearDragForce composed via createPlanarProjectileModel; measured Euler slope over h=0.02..0.00125 is 1.00+/-0.05 with monotonically shrinking errors.

tasks.title: Golden analytic references module: drag-free parabola, linear-drag (3.6)–(3.7), terminal-velocity drop
tasks.validation: reference self-tests (e.g., ẏ of formula satisfies ODE symbolically-sampled to 1e-12)
tasks.notes: packages/validation/src/analytic-references.ts (per the §2.2 package table's 'Analytical reference solutions... @ballista/validation' -- distinct from P2.07's generic measureConvergence harness, which stays in solverkit). Each of the three references (createDragFreeParabolaReference, createLinearDragReference, createTerminalVelocityDropReference) bundles a real Model+EvalContext with a hand-derived closed-form state(t) AND derivative(t). Rather than finite-differencing state(t) (which floors out well above 1e-12), the self-test compares the hand-derived derivative(t) directly against model.rhs(t, state(t), ., ctx) -- i.e. checks the analytic formula against the actual simulated force models, not just its own algebra -- at 6 sampled t per reference, to |diff| < 1e-12 (typically ~1e-15, machine precision). Terminal-velocity-drop in particular cross-checks eq. 3.10 against the real QuadraticDragForce: launched exactly at v_T, dvy/dt must come out ~0 from gravity+drag genuinely canceling, not by construction. Convergence tests (P2.09+) should now import these instead of redefining scenarios inline as P2.07's test did.

tasks.title: Generic explicit-RK kernel parameterized by Butcher tableau
tasks.validation: reproduces P2.06/10/11 bit-identically via tableaux
tasks.notes: Added explicit-rk-kernel.ts: ButcherTableau (c/a/b, a[i] holding only stage i's strictly-lower-triangular j<i row), stepExplicitRK (generic multi-stage kernel), and ExplicitRKStepper (a Stepper wrapping any tableau). Bit-identical reproduction of the hand-written P2.06/10/11 steppers isn't automatic from mathematical equivalence alone -- it requires matching their exact floating-point operation order: the stage increment is accumulated as a single weighted sum before the one multiply-by-h (y[i] + h*(b1*k1+b2*k2), not (y[i]+h*b1*k1)+h*b2*k2, which rounds differently), and each a_ij term is formed as h*a_ij*k_j[i] left-to-right. Also added EULER/MIDPOINT/HEUN/RK4_TABLEAU constants (RK4's ready for P2.13). explicit-rk-kernel.test.ts (6 tests): generic-via-tableau output matches ExplicitEulerStepper/MidpointRK2Stepper/HeunRK2Stepper with === (not toBeCloseTo) across 5 random states x 3 step sizes each (validation criterion); an integrate() end-to-end check against the decay-model closed-form Euler product; init-before-step guard; and a bonus RK4_TABLEAU convergence-harness check (slope 4.00+/-0.1 on the linear-drag benchmark) previewing P2.13.

tasks.title: Order-condition checker: numerically verify (4.7)-style conditions for stored tableaux to p=4/5
tasks.validation: RK4 passes 8/8 order-4 conditions; corrupted tableau caught
tasks.notes: Added order-condition-checker.ts: checkOrderConditions(tableau,tol) numerically evaluates all 8 rooted-tree order conditions the blueprint enumerates for |tau|<=4 (eq. 4.7: sum b_i=1; sum b_i c_i=1/2; the two order-3 trees sum b_i c_i^2=1/3 and sum b_i a_ij c_j=1/6; the four order-4 trees sum b_i c_i^3=1/4, sum b_i c_i a_ij c_j=1/8, sum b_i a_ij c_j^2=1/12, sum b_i a_ij a_jl c_l=1/24), tagging each with its tree order and target; verifiesOrder(tableau,claimedOrder,tol) filters to treeOrder<=claimedOrder and checks all satisfied. a_ij is read via an aEntry helper that treats ButcherTableau's ragged strictly-lower-triangular storage (a[i] holds only its j<i entries) as 0 for j>=i, so the conditions work directly against explicit-rk-kernel.ts's existing tableau representation with no new storage format. Order-5 conditions (9 further trees) have no closed form in the blueprint text and aren't needed until P2.24's DOPRI5 lands, so they're explicitly out of scope (documented in the module comment) rather than guessed at. order-condition-checker.test.ts (5 tests): RK4 passes all 8/8 (validation criterion); Euler passes only its order-1 condition and fails every higher one; midpoint/Heun pass order<=2 but fail order-3/4; a corrupted RK4 tableau (b[2] perturbed by 1e-3) is caught by verifiesOrder returning false (validation criterion); every condition's reported target matches the exact rational constant.

tasks.title: Semi-implicit (symplectic) Euler stepper using `model.partitions`
tasks.validation: slope 1; bounded energy oscillation on gravity-only (max |ΔE/E| non-growing over 100 periods of Stage-B pendulum fixture or long lofted shot)
tasks.notes: Added semi-implicit-euler-stepper.ts: SemiImplicitEulerStepper (eq. 4.3) updates each model.partitions p-channel (velocity) from the OLD state's rhs, then updates its paired q-channel (position) using the freshly updated velocity directly (dq_k/dt is assumed to equal the paired p_k channel's value exactly, true for a mechanical position/velocity split). Also added partitions:{q:[x,y],p:[vx,vy]} to createPlanarProjectileModel (planar-projectile-model.ts), which nothing had populated before this task. Worked out by hand (documented in both files) that the blueprint's literal 'gravity-only lofted shot' validation surface doesn't actually demonstrate boundedness: pure uniform gravity is a linear, unbounded, non-periodic potential, so solving the exact discrete recurrence in closed form shows BOTH explicit and symplectic Euler accumulate the SAME O(h^2*k) energy drift magnitude on a single ballistic arc (opposite sign) -- symplectic Euler's bounded-energy guarantee is specific to bounded/periodic orbits, which a linear potential doesn't have. semi-implicit-euler-stepper.test.ts (9 tests) reflects this: one-step exact recurrence check; info/init/step guard tests; slope 1.00+/-0.05 on the linear-drag benchmark (validation criterion); a planar-projectile partitions-shape check; a gravity-only test verifying the measured energy drift matches the derived +/-0.5*m*g^2*h^2*nSteps closed form for explicit vs. symplectic (proving the implementation is correct rather than asserting a boundedness property that doesn't hold there); and -- since the Stage-B pendulum model itself doesn't exist yet (P4.31) -- a harmonic-oscillator fixture (a bounded periodic system standing in for the pendulum's small-oscillation limit) run for 100 periods, showing symplectic Euler's late-period energy error stays within ~20x of its early-period error and under 1%, while explicit Euler's grows >1000x by comparison (the actual bounded-vs-secular contrast the task is teaching).
